text: ↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇱🇮🇷 The Israeli Air Force, acting on military intelligence, announced that it struck the largest petrochemical complex in Iran, located in the Assaluyeh area. It alleged that the facility was responsible for producing and exporting chemicals for the "regime’s"…" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israel is systematically dismantling the IRGC’s money machine by destroying factories and eliminating fighters and senior commanders. He noted that he spoke again with U.S. President Trump, thanking him for the heroic rescue of a US air crew member, and that Trump praised Israeli assistance, calling Israel “great.” “We destroyed the largest petrochemical plant in Iran today,” Netanyahu said, adding that “Iran is no longer the same Iran, Israel is not the same Israel. Israel is stronger than ever, and the Iranian terror regime is weaker than ever. The secret? Faith and power. We have both in abundance.” @wfwitness
Reporter: How would it not be a war crime to strike Iran’s bridges and power plants? Trump: They’re animals.
🌟 Hezbollah targets a Merkava tank and a Namira vehicle belonging to the Israeli enemy army in the town of Rashaf, southern Lebanon.
🇺🇸 ⚡️ 🇮🇷 Trump’s wife Melania on Iranian children facing war: All of this is happening for their future, so they will be safe in the years to come.

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷U.S. President Trump will continue the conflict with Iran if they don't meet his demands. Reporter: "I believe Iran does not meet your demands, mister president. Are you willing to continue the war?" Trump: "Well, you have to watch. The answer is yes…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. President Trump on Iran's latest proposal: Reporter: "What do you think of the latest proposal?" Trump: "They've made a proposal, and it's a significant proposal. It's a significant step. It's not good enough, but it's a very significant step." @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇱🇮🇷Tasnim News Agency: U.S. and Israeli fighter jets struck petrochemical plants in Asaluyeh, including the Jam and Damavand facilities. The Mobin and Damavand companies, which supplied electricity, water and oxygen to the area’s petrochemicals, were…" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 The Israeli Air Force, acting on military intelligence, announced that it struck the largest petrochemical complex in Iran, located in the Assaluyeh area. It alleged that the facility was responsible for producing and exporting chemicals for the "regime’s" armed forces, including the nuclear ministry and the IRGC. With this strike, the IDF states that it has now hit both of Iran’s main petrochemical complexes, putting more than 85% of Iran’s petrochemical exports out of use. It also alleged that the site produced materials for explosives and propellant for ballistic missiles and other weapons. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇱🇧🇬🇧 Israeli channel 14: Hezbollah launched a coast-to-sea missile at a British warship about 70 miles off the coast of Lebanon after mistaking it for an Israeli vessel. Israeli assessments indicate the ship sustained damage. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 Times of Israel : The Israeli military is investigating the possibility that Hezbollah targeted a foreign vessel off the coast of Lebanon yesterday. On Sunday, Hezbollah claimed to have targeted an Israeli Navy vessel using a cruise missile. The group said "a direct hit was confirmed" on the ship, 68 nautical miles off the Lebanese coast, "after hours of tracking the target." The IDF denies the claim, saying "this is a false report" and "no Israeli vessel was hit." "We emphasize that there was no threat to an Israeli vessel or in its vicinity at any stage," the military says. A military source says that it is possible that Hezbollah fired on a foreign vessel, which was not hit. The matter is under review by the IDF. @wfwitness
🌟 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 Israeli media: A drone struck a gathering of soldiers on the northern border in areas near the western sector of southern Lebanon.

Are you willing to continue the war?" Trump: "Well, you have to watch. The answer is yes, but you have to watch." @wfwitness

They'll have no power plants. They'll have no anything. I won't go further because there are other things that are worse than those two. And we might have, well, the thing, if I had my choice, what would I like to do? Take the oil because it's there for the taking. There's not a thing they can do about it. Unfortunately, the American people would like to see us come home. If it were up to me, I'd take the oil. I'd keep the oil. I would make plenty of money.“ tweet

On Sunday, Hezbollah claimed to have targeted an Israeli Navy vessel using a cruise missile. The terror group said "a direct hit was confirmed" on the ship, 68 nautical miles off the Lebanese coast, "after hours of tracking the target." The IDF denies the claim, saying "this is a false report" and "no Israeli vessel was hit." "We emphasize that there was no threat to an Israeli vessel or in its vicinity at any stage," the military says. Status-6 (War & Military News) At the same time, that might be the first known appearance of Swedish-supplied ground-based launcher of RBS-15 land-attack/anti-ship cruise missiles in Ukraine. https://twitter.com/Archer83Able/status/2041176018833080372/video/1 Footage from a comprehensive Ukrainian attack on Russia-occupied "Sivash" gas and oil drilling platform located in northern part of the Black Sea that took place last night. For the strikes on this object, which is a part of the offshore drilling rigs known as "Boyko Towers," the Ukrainians employed Swedish-supplied RBS-15 anti-ship/land-attack cruise missiles, one-way attack UAVs, unmanned surface vessels (USVs) carrying explosives, as well as a USV equipped with a machine gun (!). ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 The IDF announced that reserve troops from the 769th Brigade, operating under the 91st Division, are continuing targeted ground operations in southern Lebanon. During the activity, forces located underground living quarters containing military equipment and weapons, including vests, RPG rockets, and explosive devices. A flag belonging to the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) was also reportedly found at the site. The IDF added that additional searches led to the discovery of several weapons stockpiles, including AK-47 rifles, RPG launchers, sniper rifles, and equipment intended for prolonged stays. According to the IDF, troops also identified an alleged Hezbollah observation post in the area and dismantled it using tank fire. @wfwitness
